CVE-2019-10906.491acb07b4c58f063de77e8824b868c9f14e45f8:
Merge bookinfo from master to release 1 2 (#15261)

* Jinja version pined in bookinfo has CVE-2019-10906 (#14255)

requirements.txt containing this vuln triggered a github alert this
morning.

* Fix productpage, mysqldb and bump images version (#14239)

* add hostname variables, mistakenly removed by the previous merge

* bump the images version to 1.14.0

* change the egress rule for google APIs to use HTTPS

* Revert "change the egress rule for google APIs to use HTTPS"

This reverts commit 08b9c72eff699f9fb94e314a39454e9d7fb548b9.

* change port in TLS origination (443 -> 80)

* revert mysql image back to 1.12.0 for testing

* Revert "revert mysql image back to 1.12.0 for testing"

This reverts commit 9dd58ef246cd4f1ef963f5ef1f30e84356d6b874.

* send mysql query in a callback to connection.connect()

* use tcp protocol for mysql port

otherwise it does not work, mentioned in https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/10062#issuecomment-489516364

* use the old default mysql authentication plugin mysql_native_password

the new plugin, caching_sha2_password is not yet supported by node.js

the situation is described in this Stack Overflow answer - https://stackoverflow.com/a/50377944

* Change the version tag for base image from latest (#13875)

The bookinfo-review sample app uses websphere-liberty:latest to create a base
image. This PR changes the latest tag to reflect an actual version. As a best
practise, we do not want to use the latest tag since that could point to different
versions as the release of the base image changes.

Partially Fixes:#13262

* Modify `docker build` commands in the sample apps Dockerfile to use `--pull` tag (#13716)

Update the docker build commands in the sample apps Dockerfile to always pull newer
version of images to get the latest security updates of base images. Without this
option, it uses the locally downloaded image with the same tag if one exists.

Fixes Bug: #13714

* Adapt permissions to make productpage microservice work as non-root. (#14943)

Minimal change to demo microservice to make the image also runnable for different users.

Closes #14667

* Update bookinfo images to 1.15.0 (#14995)

This updates the bookinfo images to version 1.15.0.

Signed-off-by: Venil Noronha <veniln@vmware.com>

CVE-2016-7076.5e84dc4b360d0e1571c2a9823d5bfcbf73347bbb:
Fix security vulnerabilities for istio components (#14615)

The following security vulnerability was discovered during our
image scanning - CVE-2016-7076, related to installing the 'sudo'
package (https://usn.ubuntu.com/3968-1/). This has been fixed
in the latest 1.2rc0 release by building new images with the
latest sudo packages for the xenial version

The fix in this PR includes minor updates to Dockerfile for xenial
and bionic versio:
- cleaning up the apt-cache folder after installing packages for
  docker/.Dockerfile.xenial_debug (already done in
  docker/Dockerfile.bionic_debug)
- upgrading the base packages using `apt-get upgrade` for
  docker/Dockerfile.bionic_debug (already done in
  docker/Dockerfile.bionic_debug). Without this step, the image was
  showing security vulnerabilities.

Fixes Bug:#14220

(cherry picked from commit 4348ec77ca260c9d2dc6523630bdd7b69dca2830)
CVE-2016-7076.0a15c7e41bafb232e522eeb89ef3cf4ba1c5741e:

The following security vulnerability was discovered during our
image scanning - CVE-2016-7076, related to installing the 'sudo'
package (https://usn.ubuntu.com/3968-1/). This has been fixed
in the latest 1.2rc0 release by building new images with the
latest sudo packages for the xenial version

The fix in this PR includes minor updates to Dockerfile for xenial
and bionic versio:
- cleaning up the apt-cache folder after installing packages for
  docker/.Dockerfile.xenial_debug (already done in
  docker/Dockerfile.bionic_debug)
- upgrading the base packages using `apt-get upgrade` for
  docker/Dockerfile.bionic_debug (already done in
  docker/Dockerfile.bionic_debug). Without this step, the image was
  showing security vulnerabilities.

Fixes Bug:#14220
